title: gh#76: R6.1 — Push rate-limit DB loaders to @sms/shared/rate-limit/loaders.ts
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/76.
Backlog section: Candidate 6 — Rate-limit loader dedupe.

## Context

Candidate 6 closes the deliberate duplication between `api/services/rate-limit.service.ts` and `worker/rate-limit.ts`. Both files have *identical* loader functions (`loadTwitterUsage` ≡ `loadWorkerUsage`, plus the LinkedIn / Facebook window loaders). The worker comment explicitly says: *"We deliberately duplicate this read logic instead of importing from `@sms/api` (revision Blocker 4)"*.

The pure budget calculator already lives in `@sms/shared/rate-limit/check-budget.ts`. This PR moves the loaders alongside.

See [BACKLOG.md](../blob/main/BACKLOG.md) → Architectural Deepening → Candidate 6.

## Scope

- Add `packages/shared/src/rate-limit/loaders.ts`:
  - `loadTwitterUsage(db: Db | WorkerDb, profileId, now?): Promise<UsageSnapshot>`
  - `loadLinkedInWindowUsage(db, profileId, now?): Promise<PlatformWindowSnapshot>`
  - `loadFacebookWindowUsage(db, profileId, now?): Promise<PlatformWindowSnapshot>`
  - Loaders accept the union `Db | WorkerDb` so both packages can call them
- `@sms/shared/package.json` gains `drizzle-orm` as a runtime dep (currently only a transitive type-import). This is consistent with the principle recorded in ADR-0007: shared MAY have drizzle for inherently I/O-bound utilities, but NOT for pure domain logic (the Post aggregate stays pure)
- Switch `packages/api/src/services/rate-limit.service.ts`:
  - Delete `loadTwitterUsage`, `loadLinkedInUsage`, `loadFacebookUsage` (now imported from shared)
  - `checkTwitterBudgetWithDb`, `checkLinkedInBudgetWithDb`, `checkFacebookBudgetWithDb` keep their existing shape but compose the shared loader
- Switch `packages/worker/src/rate-limit.ts`:
  - Delete `loadWorkerUsage`, `loadLinkedInWindowUsage`, `loadFacebookWindowUsage`
  - `checkBudgetForWorker`, `checkLinkedInBudgetForWorker`, `checkFacebookBudgetForWorker` compose the shared loader
  - Delete the local `COUNTED_STATUSES` constant — now lives in shared loader
  - Remove the *"We deliberately duplicate"* doc comment (now obsolete)
- Update existing tests — most should keep working, may need to import paths updated

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the pure calculator (`check-budget.ts`) — it already lives in shared and is well-shaped
- Changing the budget-check API (the `checkXBudgetWithDb` wrappers stay package-local, only loaders move)
- LinkedIn/Facebook usage loaders in `api/services/rate-limit.service.ts` that aren't duplicated (verify with diff — keep package-local if no worker counterpart)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared build` and test pass
- `pnpm --filter @sms/api build` and test pass
- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- `rg "publishedCount: sql<number>\`count" packages/api packages/worker --type ts` returns matches only in shared (the count-loading SQL now lives in one place)
- The `COUNTED_STATUSES` array exists in exactly one file (`@sms/shared/rate-limit/loaders.ts`)

## References

- ADRs: [0007](../blob/main/docs/adr/0007-drizzle-in-shared-allowed-for-io-utilities-not-pure-logic.md)
- Depends on: nothing (independent of other candidates)

history.reasoning: Current evidence still matches the finding. `packages/shared/src/rate-limit/loaders.ts` does not exist; `find packages/shared/src/rate-limit -maxdepth 1 -type f -print` shows only `check-budget.ts`. The package metadata path in the finding, `packages/shared/src/package.json`, is stale/nonexistent, and the real `packages/shared/package.json` still has no `drizzle-orm` dependency. The duplicated DB loader logic remains in both places: `packages/api/src/services/rate-limit.service.ts` still defines `COUNTED_STATUSES`, `loadTwitterUsage`, `loadLinkedInUsage`, and `loadFacebookUsage`, including the `publishedCount: sql<number>` count query; `packages/worker/src/rate-limit.ts` still defines its own `COUNTED_STATUSES`, `loadWorkerUsage`, `loadLinkedInWindowUsage`, and `loadFacebookWindowUsage`, and still contains the obsolete 'We deliberately duplicate this read logic' comment. The acceptance grep returns matches in api and worker, not shared. Typecheck evidence: `pnpm --filter @sms/shared typecheck` passes; `@sms/api` and `@sms/worker` typechecks currently fail on unrelated files (`post-crud.service.ts` and `post-lifecycle.impl.ts`), so the requested package acceptance gates are not clean, but the static evidence is sufficient to classify this issue as still open.
commands: rg -n -e 'COUNTED_STATUSES' -e 'loadTwitterUsage' -e 'loadWorkerUsage' -e 'loadLinkedInUsage' -e 'loadLinkedInWindowUsage' -e 'loadFacebookUsage' -e 'loadFacebookWindowUsage' -e 'publishedCount: sql<number>`count' -e 'We deliberately duplicate' packages/api packages/shared packages/worker --type ts

history.reasoning: Current code shows the finding has been addressed. `packages/shared/src/rate-limit/loaders.ts` now exists and defines `loadTwitterUsage`, `loadLinkedInWindowUsage`, `loadFacebookWindowUsage`, and the sole `COUNTED_STATUSES`. `packages/shared/package.json` exports `./rate-limit/loaders` and has `drizzle-orm` as a runtime dependency; the original `packages/shared/src/package.json` evidence path is stale/nonexistent. `packages/api/src/services/rate-limit.service.ts` and `packages/worker/src/rate-limit.ts` now import the shared loaders instead of defining duplicate DB loaders, and the obsolete worker duplication comment is gone. Searches for the counted-status constant and rate-limit DB fields show the loader SQL now lives in shared, not in api/worker. Typecheck and targeted test commands were attempted, but this workspace install is incomplete: package scripts fail because `tsc` and `vitest` are not linked/found, and the `.pnpm` package dirs checked are empty, so dynamic verification could not run. Static current-code evidence satisfies the acceptance criteria.
commands: pwd && rg --files packages/api/src packages/shared packages/worker/src | sort

rg -n -e 'COUNTED_STATUSES' -e 'loadTwitterUsage' -e 'loadWorkerUsage' -e 'loadLinkedInUsage' -e 'loadLinkedInWindowUsage' -e 'loadFacebookUsage' -e 'loadFacebookWindowUsage' -e 'publishedCount: sql<number>`count' -e 'We deliberately duplicate' packages/api packages/shared packages/worker --type ts

test -f packages/shared/src/rate-limit/loaders.ts && sed -n '1,260p' packages/shared/src/rate-limit/loaders.ts; test -f packages/shared/src/package.json && sed -n '1,160p' packages/shared/src/package.json; sed -n '1,200p' packages/shared/package.json
rg -n 'publishedCount: sql<number>`count' packages/api packages/worker packages/shared --type ts

rg -n -e 'published_at >=' -e 'monthly_tweet_budget' -e 'linkedin_daily_limit' -e 'facebook_hourly_limit' -e 'linkedin_daily_count' -e 'facebook_hourly_count' -e 'status in \(' packages/api/src packages/worker/src packages/shared/src --type ts
